 {"id":"bd-1pb","title":"Perf: Baseline + optimization plan (alien-grade rigor)","description":"# Goal
Establish a **profile-driven optimization loop** for the PiJS runtime and connectors with reproducible outputs.

# Method (Extreme Optimization)
1) Baseline: `hyperfine --warmup 3 --runs 10` on key extension workloads.
2) Profile: `cargo flamegraph` for CPU, heaptrack for allocations.
3) Prove: golden outputs + isomorphism checklist for every change.
4) Implement: only opportunities with score ≥ 2.0.
5) Verify: sha256 of golden outputs.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Benchmark scripts for representative extensions.
- Opportunity matrix with impact/confidence/effort.
- Initial flamegraph and top hotspots.
- Log artifacts per run (JSONL + summary table).

# Output
- Short performance report tied to `bd-btq` success criteria.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:23:45.780267200Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T18:08:25.401857386Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1pb","depends_on_id":"bd-2ds","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:26.236917347Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1pb","depends_on_id":"bd-2ke","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:25.317622080Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1pb","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:25:25.777714572Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1pf","title":"Set up VCR-style test infrastructure for provider streaming","description":"# Set up VCR-style test infrastructure for provider streaming

## Goal
Create infrastructure to record and replay real API responses for provider testing.

## Background
VCR (Video Cassette Recorder) testing pattern:
1. First run: Make real API call, record response to file
2. Subsequent runs: Replay recorded response, no network

This gives us:
- Real response format testing (not simplified mocks)
- Deterministic CI (no flaky network tests)
- Real error response testing (rate limits, auth failures)

## Implementation

### Recording Mode
```rust
// tests/vcr/mod.rs

pub struct VcrRecorder {
    cassette_path: PathBuf,
    mode: VcrMode,
}

pub enum VcrMode {
    Record,    // Make real calls, save to cassette
    Playback,  // Replay from cassette, fail if not found
    Auto,      // Playback if exists, else record
}

impl VcrRecorder {
    pub fn new(test_name: &str) -> Self { ... }

    pub async fn request(&self, req: Request) -> Response {
        match self.mode {
            VcrMode::Record => self.record_and_return(req).await,
            VcrMode::Playback => self.playback(req),
            VcrMode::Auto => ...
        }
    }
}
```

### Cassette Format
```json
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "test_name": "anthropic_streaming_text",
  "recorded_at": "2026-02-02T...",
  "interactions": [
    {
      "request": {
        "method": "POST",
        "url": "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages",
        "headers": { ... },
        "body": { ... }
      },
      "response": {
        "status": 200,
        "headers": { ... },
        "body_chunks": [
          "event: message_start\ndata: {...}\n\n",
          "event: content_block_delta\ndata: {...}\n\n",
          ...
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### SSE Streaming Support
For streaming responses, store individual chunks:
- Each SSE event as separate chunk
- Preserve timing information (optional replay delay)
- Support partial responses (stream interruption)

### Sensitive Data Redaction
```rust
pub fn redact_cassette(cassette: &mut Cassette) {
    // Replace API keys with placeholder
    cassette.redact_header("x-api-key", "[REDACTED]");
    cassette.redact_header("authorization", "[REDACTED]");

    // Replace sensitive response data
    cassette.redact_body_field("api_key", "[REDACTED]");
}
```

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) to log record/playback actions.
- Log cassette path, mode, request summary (method/url), and redaction summary.
- On playback mismatch, log expected vs actual interaction keys + diff hints.

## Files to Create
- tests/vcr/mod.rs - VCR infrastructure
- tests/vcr/cassette.rs - Cassette format/loading
- tests/vcr/recorder.rs - Recording logic
- tests/vcr/playback.rs - Playback logic
- tests/fixtures/vcr/ - Directory for cassette files

## Testing the VCR System
- Unit test: Load cassette from JSON
- Unit test: Match request to interaction
- Unit test: Redaction works
- Integration: Record + playback cycle

## Environment Variables
- VCR_MODE=record|playback|auto
- VCR_CASSETTE_DIR=path/to/cassettes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] VCR infrastructure compiles
- [ ] Recording mode saves cassettes
- [ ] Playback mode replays cassettes
- [ ] SSE streaming supported
- [ ] Sensitive data redacted
- [ ] Record/playback actions logged with detail
- [ ] CI uses playback mode only

 {"id":"bd-1uk","title":"Connector: process/env/path/crypto/time minimal APIs","description":"# Goal
Fill remaining minimal OS surface in PiJS without pulling in Node/Bun.

# Scope / Deliverables
- `pi.env.get` (whitelisted keys only).
- `pi.process.cwd`, `pi.process.args` (read-only).
- `pi.path` helpers (join, basename, normalize).
- `pi.crypto` subset (hashing, random bytes) backed by Rust.
- `pi.time` (monotonic now, sleep via timers).
- Structured logs for env access and crypto ops (non-secret metadata only).

# Security Invariants
- No ability to read arbitrary env vars.
- Randomness and hashing are constant-time and auditable.

# Tests
- Unit tests for env allowlist enforcement.
- Deterministic hashing for test seeds (if provided).
- E2E security suite validates env denial.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:47.943408211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T18:07:24.845953399Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uk","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:24:57.190277129Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1uk","depends_on_id":"bd-h04","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:24:57.652329703Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1uy","title":"Workstream: Rust extension runtime compatibility complete","description":"Purpose:
- Implement extension runtime support in pi_agent_rust so extensions run *as-is* with correct protocol, capabilities, and event wiring.

Outputs (artifacts):
- Extension manifest loader + compatibility scanner.
- WASM host + WIT bindings; JS compatibility pipeline.
- Runtime wiring for tool_call, slash_command, event_hooks.
- Configurable policy modes (strict/prompt/permissive) with audit logging.

Definition of done:
- The runtime can load sample extensions and expose their tools/commands.
- Capability policy is enforced consistently and logged.
- No modifications to extension source are required (as-is).

 {"id":"bd-26s","title":"Workstream: Comprehensive Test Coverage (No Mocks)","description":"# Workstream: Comprehensive Test Coverage (No Mocks)

## Goal
Achieve 95%+ test coverage using real implementations wherever possible, with
detailed logging for E2E integration tests. Eliminate reliance on mocks for
core functionality testing.

## Background
Current test coverage analysis reveals critical gaps:

### Well Tested (Good)
- Built-in Tools: 100% coverage (122 conformance fixture cases)
- Session Tree Logic: 11 tests covering branching/navigation
- SSE Parser: 9 tests covering all edge cases
- Auth Flow: 3 tests for PKCE/OAuth setup

### Critical Gaps (Bad)
- **Provider Streaming**: 0 tests with real API/streaming (only unit tests)
- **Interactive TUI**: 2 tests out of 2842 lines (<0.1%)
- **CLI/main.rs**: 0 tests for 1581 lines
- **Compaction**: 0 tests for 903 lines
- **Configuration**: 0 tests for 332 lines
- **Session Persistence**: Only serialization tested, not full I/O cycle

### What "No Mocks" Means
- Provider tests: Use VCR-style recorded responses, not fake providers
- File tests: Use real temp directories (already done)
- CLI tests: Use actual binary execution
- TUI tests: Use terminal emulator or snapshot testing
- Network tests: Use local test server or recorded responses

## Testing Strategy

### 1. Provider Streaming Tests (VCR Style)
Record real API responses and replay them:
- No external network calls during CI
- Real response parsing (not simplified mocks)
- Error scenarios from real API errors

### 2. E2E Integration Tests
Full workflow tests with verbose logging:
- Start pi binary with arguments
- Verify output and exit codes
- Check session files created
- Log every step for debugging

### 3. Snapshot Testing for TUI
Capture terminal output and compare:
- Render TUI components to string
- Compare against golden snapshots
- Update snapshots on intentional changes

### 4. Property-Based Testing
Use proptest for edge cases:
- Truncation with random inputs
- Session serialization round-trips
- Path resolution edge cases

### 5. Compaction Tests
- Window selection + summary insertion
- Tool/thinking/image content handling
- Session invariants preserved after compaction

### 6. Configuration Tests
- Config discovery + precedence rules
- Env + CLI override behavior
- Invalid config errors with actionable messages

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Provider streaming tested with recorded responses
- [ ] TUI logic tested (state transitions, rendering)
- [ ] CLI argument parsing fully tested
- [ ] Compaction logic tested
- [ ] Configuration loading tested
- [ ] Session persistence cycles tested
- [ ] E2E tests with verbose logging exist
- [ ] CI runs all tests in <5 minutes

## Files to Create
- tests/provider_streaming.rs - VCR-style provider tests
- tests/e2e_cli.rs - CLI integration tests
- tests/tui_snapshot.rs - TUI snapshot tests
- tests/compaction.rs - Compaction pipeline tests
- tests/config_loading.rs - Config discovery/override tests
- tests/fixtures/vcr/*.json - Recorded API responses

## Dependencies
None (can start immediately, independent of HTTP migration)","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:34:05.966405126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:46:20.507382886Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-26s","depends_on_id":"bd-2qk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:34:05.973753960Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2xc","title":"Unit tests: PiJS stdlib shims + JS boundary","description":"# Goal
Comprehensive **unit tests** for PiJS shims and the Rust↔QuickJS boundary.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Test `pi.fs`, `pi.http`, `pi.path`, `pi.env`, `pi.crypto`, `pi.time` shims.
- Verify error mapping (timeouts, denied capability, IO errors).
- Confirm consistent behavior with deterministic seeds.
- Include structured log assertions for key flows.

# Why

 {"id":"bd-30u","title":"Record VCR cassettes for Anthropic provider scenarios","description":"# Record VCR cassettes for Anthropic provider scenarios

## Goal
Create recorded cassettes for all Anthropic provider test scenarios.

## Scenarios to Record

### Happy Path Scenarios
1. **Simple text response** - Single text block, stop reason "stop"
2. **Multi-paragraph response** - Multiple text deltas
3. **Extended thinking** - thinking_start/delta/end + text response
4. **Tool call single** - Single tool_call in response
5. **Tool call multiple** - Multiple tool_calls in single response
6. **Tool result processing** - tool_result message handling

### Error Scenarios
7. **Rate limit (429)** - With retry-after header
8. **Auth failure (401)** - Invalid API key
9. **Forbidden (403)** - Resource access denied
10. **Bad request (400)** - Malformed request
11. **Server error (500)** - Internal server error
12. **Overloaded (529)** - Anthropic overloaded

### Edge Cases
13. **Empty response** - No content blocks
14. **Very long response** - Hits max_tokens
15. **Unicode content** - Emoji, CJK, RTL text
16. **Large tool call args** - Big JSON in arguments
17. **Stream interruption** - Partial response simulation

### Extended Thinking Variants
18. **Thinking only** - No final text, just thinking
19. **Thinking with tool calls** - Thinking then tool use
20. **Thinking budget exceeded** - Hits thinking token limit

## Recording Process
```bash
# Set API key (not committed)
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...

# Record mode
VCR_MODE=record cargo test provider_streaming::anthropic

# Verify cassettes created
ls tests/fixtures/vcr/anthropic_*.json
```

## Logging Requirements
- Use TestLogger (bd-3ml) during recording runs.
- Log cassette name/path, model, prompt summary hash, and redaction summary.
- Capture command line + env (redacted) used to record each cassette.

## Determinism + Redaction
- Keep prompts/tool schemas identical across recordings; store prompt hashes in a manifest.
- Ensure API keys and any sensitive fields are redacted.
- Record with fixed model versions + params (temperature, max_tokens, thinking level).

## Files Created
- tests/fixtures/vcr/anthropic_simple_text.json
- tests/fixtures/vcr/anthropic_extended_thinking.json
- tests/fixtures/vcr/anthropic_tool_call.json
- tests/fixtures/vcr/anthropic_rate_limit.json
- ... (20 cassettes total)
- tests/fixtures/vcr/anthropic_manifest.json (scenario → prompt hash + params)

## Dependencies
- bd-1pf (VCR infrastructure must exist)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 20 cassettes recorded
- [ ] All scenarios covered
- [ ] Sensitive data redacted
- [ ] Cassettes valid JSON
- [ ] Manifest includes prompt hashes + params
- [ ] Playback matches recording
- [ ] Recording logs captured with redacted env","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:34:42.752221104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T05:41:46.052453953Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-30u","depends_on_id":"bd-1pf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:35:07.519020783Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-30u","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T03:34:42.762776734Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-gi3","title":"Workstream: HTTP Migration to asupersync","description":"# Workstream: HTTP Migration to asupersync

## Goal
Replace reqwest + tokio with asupersync HTTP client across all providers, enabling:
- Structured concurrency with cancel-correct operations
- Deterministic testing via LabRuntime
- Removal of tokio dependency (smaller binary, simpler runtime)
- Unified HTTP/TLS/SQLite stack from asupersync

## Background
asupersync is a sibling library at ../asupersync providing:
- Capability-based context (Cx) for async operations
- Built-in HTTP client with rustls TLS
- Structured cancellation (no orphaned futures)
- Deterministic LabRuntime for testing

Currently, pi_agent_rust has:
- src/http/mod.rs - Module stub
- src/http/client.rs - RequestBuilder that returns "not implemented" error
- src/http/sse.rs - SSE parser ready for integration
- All 4 providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Azure) use reqwest directly

## Technical Approach
1. Implement asupersync HTTP client wrapper in src/http/client.rs
2. Create SSE streaming adapter using existing src/http/sse.rs parser
3. Migrate Anthropic provider first (most complex, has extended thinking)
4. Migrate remaining providers (OpenAI, Gemini, Azure)
5. Remove reqwest + tokio dependencies from Cargo.toml
6. Update all async code to use Cx context

## Dependencies
- asupersync must compile (currently has 3 unused import warnings, otherwise OK)
- SSE parser (src/sse.rs) already exists and is well-tested (9 tests)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All HTTP requests go through asupersync client
- [ ] SSE streaming works for all providers
- [ ] tokio removed from Cargo.toml
- [ ] reqwest removed from Cargo.toml
- [ ] Tests pass with deterministic LabRuntime
- [ ] No performance regression (benchmark streaming throughput)

## Risks
- asupersync HTTP API may differ from reqwest
- TLS certificate handling may need attention
- Streaming may have subtle behavior differences

## Files Affected
- src/http/mod.rs
- src/http/client.rs
- src/http/sse.rs
- src/providers/anthropic.rs
- src/providers/openai.rs
- src/providers/gemini.rs
- src/providers/azure.rs

- EXISTING_PI_STRUCTURE has enough detail to implement extensions/resources without consulting legacy code.
